### Post by Zanik221 on 2007-02-15

### Post by veloce on 2007-02-17
When you installed 915resolution did you leave it on automatic or have you tried manually setting the resolution?

perhaps you could post the results of:

sudo 915resolution -l

### Post by veloce on 2007-02-23
looks like you need to edit the 915resolution file as it isn't picking up the "1280x800" resolution.

I think it's /etc/default/915resolution. Comment out the "mode=auto" line and set the xres and yres lines to 1280 and 800 respectively.

---

### Post by reveiled on 2007-05-16
I have downloaded 915resolution however I am new to linux (really new)
How do I install it or enable it?

---

### Post by veloce on 2007-05-16
> **reveiled said:**
> I have downloaded 915resolution however I am new to linux (really new)
How do I install it or enable it?

The easiest install is to use synaptic package manager (from the System - Administration menu) to both download and install.  It's only little, so I suggest letting it download again.  

If you install from a downloaded .deb file, I'm not sure if the update manager will keep it up to date.

Once installed, the default settings will probably work.  If it doesn't, you need to edit the configuration file:

/etc/default/915resolution

It's not hard, but don't go there if you don't have to.

---
